title: pimelea orthia
intro: Pimelea orthia, also known as Native Rice Flower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its small white or pinkish flowers and a slender stem. The plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pimelea orthia include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. Pimelea orthia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
